task_description: Write a method in Dafny to find the index of the maximum element in an array.
verifier_error:
662_code.dfy(21,14): Error: index out of range
   |
21 | 		if a[i] > a[max_idx]
   |               ^

662_code.dfy(33,2): Error: a postcondition could not be proved on this return path
   |
33 | 	return max_idx;
   | 	^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

662_code.dfy(9,10): Related location: this is the postcondition that could not be proved
   |
9  | 	ensures forall j :: 0 <= j < a.Length ==> a[max_idx] >= a[j]
   |          ^^^^^^

662_code.dfy(33,2): Error: a postcondition could not be proved on this return path
   |
33 | 	return max_idx;
   | 	^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

662_code.dfy(7,23): Related location: this is the postcondition that could not be proved
   |
7  | 	ensures 0 <= max_idx < a.Length
   |                        ^

repair_strategy.problem_summary: Missing loop invariants cause index out of range error and prevent postconditions from being verified.

problem_details.what: The while loop lacks invariants to establish: (1) the bounds of loop variable 'i', (2) the bounds of 'max_idx', and (3) the property that max_idx points to the maximum element among those already examined. This causes an 'index out of range' error when accessing a[i] and prevents the postconditions from being proved.
problem_details.why: Without the invariant 'invariant 1 <= i <= a.Length', Dafny cannot verify that 'i' is a valid index when accessing a[i] inside the loop. Without 'invariant 0 <= max_idx < i', Dafny cannot prove that max_idx stays within array bounds. Without 'invariant forall j :: 0 <= j < i ==> a[max_idx] >= a[j]', Dafny cannot establish the relationship between max_idx and the maximum value, which is needed to prove the postcondition when the loop completes with i == a.Length.
problem_details.how: Add four loop annotations: (1) 'decreases a.Length - i' to prove termination, (2) 'invariant 1 <= i <= a.Length' to establish that i is always in valid range for array access, (3) 'invariant 0 <= max_idx < i' to ensure max_idx is within the already-examined portion, and (4) 'invariant forall j :: 0 <= j < i ==> a[max_idx] >= a[j]' to track that max_idx holds the index of the maximum value among the first i elements.
